response:
to find the denominator we must store the prime factors of the factorial.
***
we need to store the count of prime factors in one hundred factorial.
***
the number ten is made of two and five so we store this fact.
***
there are more twos than fives so we store the five count.
***
we store the number of multiples of five in the range.
***
we store the multiples of twenty five and one hundred.
***
the total count of fives is stored as twenty four.
***
after dividing we store the remaining factors in the denominator.
***
the final answer is two raised to the power of twenty four.
***
we have stored the solution for the irreducible fraction denominator.
